title: The Glass Animal
genre: Experimental Art-Pop · Trip-Pop · Chamber Soul · Fragile Electronic Jazz
concept: A private museum of one person's masks — fourteen rooms and a hallway, where every object on display is something the narrator used to survive being looked at.
statement: NoiraCiel's most intimate and least armored record — close-mic, breath audible, built from prepared piano and glass-like synths rather than drums and hooks, treating vulnerability as architecture instead of confession.
visualIdentity: A house made of glass furniture, soft interior light, porcelain and static, half-developed photographs, mirrors turned to the wall.
typographyMood: Thin-weight, fragile letterforms with generous space between characters — like type seen through frosted glass.
musicalStyle: Experimental art-pop, trip-pop, chamber soul, fragile electronic jazz. Prepared piano, glass-like synths, upright bass, brushed drums, harp fragments, muted trumpet, cello, minimal sub.
vocalDirection: Smoky fragile female/androgynous alto, close-mic, breathy, detached but emotional — never performs the pain, just lets it be audible.
negativeTags: obvious choruses for their own sake

emotionalArc.phase: Cracks and the danger of being truly seen
Grief for the self that had to perform
